name: Productive Teams
description: Which teams are the most productive and why?

blocks.title: 🏆 Most Productive Engineering Team Analysis
blocks.content:
## Methodology

To identify the most productive engineering team, we analyzed four key dimensions sourced from the **Productivity**, **Teams**, and **Employees** cubes:

1. **Productivity Score** — A composite metric that blends code output, pull request activity, and deployment frequency. This is our primary ranking signal.
2. **Total Lines of Code** — Measures raw code contribution volume.
3. **Total Pull Requests** — Reflects code review activity and collaboration velocity.
4. **Total Deployments** — Indicates how often the team ships to production.
5. **Average Happiness Index** — Team morale and satisfaction, which strongly correlates with sustainable output.

> ⚠️ Non-engineering teams (Brand, People Ops, Recruiting, Customer Success, etc.) show zero code-based metrics and are excluded from the engineering ranking for clarity.

---

## 🥇 Winner: DevOps Team

The **DevOps** team ranks **#1 in overall Productivity Score (426.4)** — the highest of all teams, including engineering-focused ones. Here's why:

| Metric | DevOps | Platform (2nd) | Backend (3rd) |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Productivity Score** | **426.4** | 383.4 | 341.4 |
| Lines of Code | 163,349 | 1,761,008 | 1,392,398 |
| Pull Requests | 3,059 | 31,827 | 24,127 |
| **Deployments** | **3,574** | **11,915** | 3,677 |
| **Happiness Index** | **8.53 / 10** | 8.37 | 8.20 |

**Why DevOps wins:** Despite having fewer raw code lines than Platform or Frontend, DevOps achieves the highest *efficiency-weighted* productivity score. Their exceptionally high happiness index (8.53) and significant deployment throughput (3,574 total) signal a well-oiled, high-morale team that ships reliably. Quality over quantity.

**Runner-up — Platform:** Platform leads in raw volume (1.76M lines of code, 31,827 PRs, 11,915 deployments), making them the highest-output team by sheer scale. They rank a strong #2 overall.

**Runner-up — Backend:** Backend posts a solid all-around performance with 1.39M lines of code, 24,127 PRs, and 3,677 deployments — consistent and balanced.

blocks.title: Overall Productivity Score by Team
blocks.content: The chart below ranks all teams by their composite **Productivity Score** (all-time). Engineering teams (those with code output) are clearly separated from non-engineering ones. DevOps holds the top spot, followed by Platform and Backend.

blocks.title: Detailed Metrics: Top Engineering Teams
blocks.content: The table below shows a full breakdown of all productivity signals across every team. Focus on the top engineering teams — DevOps, Platform, Backend, Data Engineering, QA, and Frontend — to understand the nuances of each team's performance profile.

blocks.title: Productivity Score Trend — Last 3 Months (Top Engineering Teams)
blocks.content:
This line chart shows the **month-over-month productivity score trend** for the top 6 engineering teams over the last 3 months (Dec 2025 – Feb 2026). Key observations:

- **DevOps** peaked dramatically in **January 2026 (481.4)** before settling back in February — a surge in deployments drove this spike.
- **Platform** remained remarkably consistent across all three months (~272 → 448 → 383), showing reliable, high-level output.
- **Backend** showed a strong January boost and maintained solid performance.
- **Data Engineering** and **QA** are trending upward, closing the gap in early 2026.
- **Frontend** posted its highest score in January (329.8) with a dip in February.

blocks.title: 📈 Monthly Productivity Score Trend — Top Engineering Teams
Happiness vs Productivity — The Morale Connection
blocks.content: This scatter plot reveals a clear **positive correlation between team happiness and productivity score**. Engineering teams with higher morale (happiness index ≥ 8.0) consistently score higher on productivity. DevOps sits in the top-right quadrant — both the happiest and most productive team. Non-engineering teams cluster in the lower-left, with low scores on both axes.

messages.content: Who is the most productive engineering team and why?
Let me start by discovering the available cubes to understand what data we have for analyzing engineering team productivity.

toolCalls.name: discover_cubes
discover_cubes

messages.content: I now have a great understanding of the schema. I'll run several queries in parallel to gather team-level productivity data across all key metrics.

toolCalls.name: execute_query
execute_query
